---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Cooper Joseph Sharp"
date: "2026-03-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r, Load Libraries}

# ============================================================================
# ICRG: Data Cleaning + Summary Statistics for Paper
# Author: Cooper Joseph Sharp
# Date:   2026-03-26
# ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(tidycensus)
library(knitr)
library(ggplot2)

```


```{r, Load Data}

setwd("~/Documents/GitHub/2025-ICRG")
df     <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/scoring-mastersheet.csv")
df_geo <- read.csv("derivatives/scoring/geo-variables.csv")

```


```{r, Reward Sensitivity}

# ── Reward sensitivity composite 

df$bisbas_bas <- df$bisbas_drive + df$bisbas_fun_seek + df$bisbas_reward_res

calculate_z <- function(X, X_mean, S) { (X - X_mean) / S }

df$bisbas_bas_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$bisbas_bas,
  mean(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$bisbas_bas, na.rm = TRUE)
)
df$spsrq_sr_z_score <- calculate_z(
  df$spsrq_sr,
  mean(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE),
  sd(df$spsrq_sr, na.rm = TRUE)
)

df$rs_standard <- df$bisbas_bas_z_score - df$spsrq_sr_z_score

q3 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
q1 <- quantile(df$rs_standard, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE)
iqr <- q3 - q1
df <- df[!(df$rs_standard >= q3 + 1.5 * iqr |
           df$rs_standard <= q1 - 1.5 * iqr) |
           is.na(df$rs_standard), ]

df$rew_composite <- df$spsrq_sr_z_score + df$bisbas_bas_z_score

```


```{r, Add Geo Variables from Tidy Census}

# ── Gini index by ZIP ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
gini_zip <- get_acs(
  geography = "zcta",
  variables = c(gini = "B19083_001"),
  year = 2021, survey = "acs5"
) %>%
  dplyr::select(GEOID, estimate) %>%
  rename(zipcode = GEOID, gini = estimate)

df <- df %>%
  mutate(zipcode = as.character(demo_zip_prim)) %>%
  left_join(gini_zip, by = "zipcode") %>%
  left_join(df_geo, by = "ResponseId") %>%
  mutate(across(everything(), ~ ifelse(. == -999, NA, .))) %>%
  filter(if_all(c(sogs_total, bbgs_total, mandf_casinog, mandf_onlineg,
                  TotalCrime, unemployed, gini, demo_yrs, demo_gender,
                  rew_composite, susd_depression, susd_mania,
                  scared_total, uclal_total), ~ !is.na(.)))

# ── Classification ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
df <- df %>%
  mutate(
    pg = case_when(
      sogs_total >= 1 & bbgs_total >= 4 & (mandf_casinog + mandf_onlineg) >= 1 ~ 2,
      sogs_total == 0 & bbgs_total <= 3 & (mandf_casinog + mandf_onlineg) == 0 ~ 0,
      TRUE ~ 1
    ),
    pg_label = factor(
      case_when(
        pg == 0 ~ "Non-Gambler",
        pg == 1 ~ "Recreational",
        pg == 2 ~ "Problem"
      ),
      levels = c("Non-Gambler", "Recreational", "Problem")
    ),
    income_median_split = factor(
      ifelse(ses_tpi <= 2, "Below Median (<$40k)", "Above Median (≥$40k)"),
      levels = c("Below Median (<$40k)", "Above Median (≥$40k)")
    ),
    race = factor(
      case_when(
        demo_race == 5 ~ "White",
        demo_race == 4 ~ "Black",
        demo_race == 2 ~ "Asian",
        TRUE           ~ "Other"
      ),
      levels = c("White", "Black", "Asian", "Other")
    ),
    gender_label = factor(
      demo_gender,
      levels = c(1, 2),
      labels = c("Male", "Female")
    )
  )

```


```{r, Export Data}

write.csv(df, "derivatives/scoring/analytic-sample.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Cleaned CSV exported:", nrow(df), "rows\n\n")

```


```{r, Exclusions and Demographics}

# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 3. SAMPLE OVERVIEW
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  SAMPLE OVERVIEW\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("Total N:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Non-Gambler:", sum(df$pg == 0),
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", 100 * mean(df$pg == 0)), "\n")
cat("Recreational:", sum(df$pg == 1),
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", 100 * mean(df$pg == 1)), "\n")
cat("Problem:", sum(df$pg == 2),
    sprintf("(%.1f%%)", 100 * mean(df$pg == 2)), "\n\n")


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 4. TABLE 1 — DEMOGRAPHICS BY GROUP
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# ── Age ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("── Age ──\n")
df %>%
  group_by(pg_label) %>%
  summarise(
    N    = n(),
    Mean = round(mean(demo_yrs, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    SD   = round(sd(demo_yrs, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Min  = min(demo_yrs, na.rm = TRUE),
    Max  = max(demo_yrs, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% kable()

cat("\nAge ANOVA:\n")
summary(aov(demo_yrs ~ pg_label, data = df))

# ── Gender ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n── Gender ──\n")
gender_tab <- table(df$gender_label, df$pg_label)
print(gender_tab)
cat("\nGender chi-square:\n")
print(chisq.test(gender_tab))

# Gender proportions by group
df %>%
  filter(!is.na(gender_label)) %>%
  group_by(pg_label, gender_label) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(pg_label) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  kable()

# ── Race ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n── Race ──\n")
race_tab <- table(df$race, df$pg_label)
print(race_tab)
cat("\nRace chi-square:\n")
print(chisq.test(race_tab))

df %>%
  group_by(pg_label, race) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
  group_by(pg_label) %>%
  mutate(pct = round(100 * n / sum(n), 1)) %>%
  kable()

# ── Income ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n── Income ──\n")
income_tab <- table(df$income_median_split, df$pg_label)
print(income_tab)
cat("\nIncome chi-square:\n")
print(chisq.test(income_tab))

# ── Years of Education ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
cat("\n── Years of Education ──\n")
df %>%
  group_by(pg_label) %>%
  summarise(
    Mean = round(mean(ses_yoe, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    SD   = round(sd(ses_yoe, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% kable()

cat("\nYOE ANOVA:\n")
summary(aov(ses_yoe ~ pg_label, data = df))


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 5. TABLE 2 — KEY MEASURES BY GROUP (M, SD, group test)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

continuous_vars <- c(
  "rew_composite",
  "dospert_soc", "dospert_ethic", "dospert_inv",
  "dospert_hs", "dospert_rec",
  "susd_depression", "susd_mania",
  "scared_total", "uclal_total",
  "planfulness_mean",
  "fevs_total", "usidep_total",
  "TotalCrime", "unemployed", "gini",
  "sogs_total", "bbgs_total"
)

var_labels <- c(
  "Reward Sensitivity (composite)",
  "DOSPERT Social", "DOSPERT Ethical", "DOSPERT Investment",
  "DOSPERT Health/Safety", "DOSPERT Recreational",
  "Depression (SUSD)", "Mania (SUSD)",
  "Anxiety (SCARED)", "Loneliness (UCLA)",
  "Planfulness",
  "Financial Exploitation (FEVS)", "Neighborhood Deprivation (USIDEP)",
  "Crime Rate", "Unemployment Rate", "Gini Index",
  "SOGS Total", "BBGS Total"
)

cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  KEY MEASURES BY GROUP\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n\n")

measures_table <- do.call(rbind, lapply(seq_along(continuous_vars), function(i) {
  var <- continuous_vars[i]
  label <- var_labels[i]

  group_stats <- df %>%
    group_by(pg_label) %>%
    summarise(
      M  = mean(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      SD = sd(.data[[var]], na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    )

  # One-way ANOVA p-value
  f_test <- tryCatch({
    fit <- aov(reformulate("pg_label", response = var), data = df)
    p <- summary(fit)[[1]][["Pr(>F)"]][1]
    round(p, 3)
  }, error = function(e) NA)

  ng  <- group_stats %>% filter(pg_label == "Non-Gambler")
  rec <- group_stats %>% filter(pg_label == "Recreational")
  pg  <- group_stats %>% filter(pg_label == "Problem")

  data.frame(
    Variable  = label,
    NG_M_SD   = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", ng$M, ng$SD),
    Rec_M_SD  = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", rec$M, rec$SD),
    PG_M_SD   = sprintf("%.2f (%.2f)", pg$M, pg$SD),
    p         = ifelse(is.na(f_test), "—",
                  ifelse(f_test < .001, "<.001", as.character(f_test))),
    row.names = NULL
  )
}))

kable(measures_table,
      col.names = c("Variable", "Non-Gambler M(SD)",
                     "Recreational M(SD)", "Problem M(SD)", "p"))


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 6. GAMBLING BEHAVIOR DESCRIPTIVES (for Table 1 or narrative)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\n── Gambling Modalities ──\n")
df %>%
  group_by(pg_label) %>%
  summarise(
    Casino_pct  = round(100 * mean(mandf_casinog > 0, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    Online_pct  = round(100 * mean(mandf_onlineg > 0, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    SOGS_mean   = round(mean(sogs_total, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    SOGS_sd     = round(sd(sogs_total, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    BBGS_mean   = round(mean(bbgs_total, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    BBGS_sd     = round(sd(bbgs_total, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>% kable()


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 7. CORRELATION MATRIX (continuous predictors)
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cor_vars <- df %>%
  dplyr::select(
    rew_composite,
    dospert_soc, dospert_ethic, dospert_inv,
    dospert_hs, dospert_rec,
    susd_depression, susd_mania,
    scared_total, uclal_total,
    planfulness_mean,
    fevs_total, usidep_total,
    TotalCrime, unemployed, gini,
    demo_yrs
  ) %>%
  drop_na()

cor_mat <- round(cor(cor_vars), 2)

cat("\n── Correlation Matrix ──\n")
kable(cor_mat)

# Flag high correlations (|r| > .60)
cat("\n── Correlations |r| > .60 ──\n")
high_cors <- which(abs(cor_mat) > 0.60 & upper.tri(cor_mat), arr.ind = TRUE)
if (nrow(high_cors) > 0) {
  for (i in 1:nrow(high_cors)) {
    cat(sprintf("  %s × %s: r = %.2f\n",
        rownames(cor_mat)[high_cors[i, 1]],
        colnames(cor_mat)[high_cors[i, 2]],
        cor_mat[high_cors[i, 1], high_cors[i, 2]]))
  }
} else {
  cat("  None\n")
}


# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# 8. SAMPLE FLOW / EXCLUSIONS
# ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

cat("\n══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("  SAMPLE FLOW\n")
cat("══════════════════════════════════════════════════\n")
cat("Total after cleaning:", nrow(df), "\n")
cat("Gender 1 (Male):",   sum(df$demo_gender == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Gender 2 (Female):", sum(df$demo_gender == 2, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Gender 3 (Other):",  sum(df$demo_gender == 3, na.rm = TRUE), "\n")
cat("Missing gender:",    sum(is.na(df$demo_gender)), "\n\n")

# Analytic N after dropping gender == 3 (used in all models)
analytic <- df %>% filter(demo_gender %in% c(1, 2))
cat("Analytic N (gender 1 or 2):", nrow(analytic), "\n")
cat("  Non-Gambler:", sum(analytic$pg == 0), "\n")
cat("  Recreational:", sum(analytic$pg == 1), "\n")
cat("  Problem:",      sum(analytic$pg == 2), "\n")

```
